axis_responsibility: Brick: declared Building Plan, step order, work kind, and required return shape remain the work contract.
Agent: returns closed AgentFact payload; the notification layer may summarize returned text but must not classify it.
Link: gate/movement/disposition facts remain Link-owned; notifier only renders observed gate/disposition evidence.
Support: reporter, sinks, run, walker, and checker pins may observe evidence and fan out support notifications without becoming source truth or Movement authority.
checker_or_verifier_plan: Extend reporter_notification_projection in kernel_checks.py, not a new standalone checker.
Pin absent/building grain parity: default policy event_kinds and Slack payloads match current three-kind notify-v2 behavior.

Pin brick grain with captured sender: building_started returns ts, raw/report-thread.jsonl records it, brick_returned request carries matching thread_ts, and text includes ① plus 받음/반환/게이트 segments with KST HH:MM.
Pin disposition_applied after hold-resume as a separate thread reply.
Pin mutation RED by relying on the captured request assertion for thread_ts; deleting the thread_ts attachment must fail the focused profile.
Extend temp-root external sink guard to all new event kinds and keep no-scheduler file list green.
Proof commands after implementation: compileall, git diff --check, focused reporter profile, mutation RED, then temp source copy bake_dashboard_data_json plus check_profile.py --all.
design_summary: Implement notify-v3 as an additive support-only brick-grain notification layer behind BRICK_REPORT_GRAIN=brick.
Default grain is building; absent/building mode must keep current event kinds and Slack payloads byte-identical.
Slack channel messages remain limited to the current three building-level kinds; brick-grain events post only as replies under the recorded building_started parent ts.
Record the building_started Slack ts/channel as a building-root support observation, preferably raw/report-thread.jsonl, after provider ok without storing response body or secrets.

Use brick_returned as the Slack-visible per-step incident reply because it is the first seam with return summary plus gate result available; keep brick_received and gate_passed as additive support event kinds without adding extra Slack replies for the same incident.
edge_cases: building_started Slack delivery fails or response lacks ts: record no parent thread; later brick events return not_attempted_missing_thread_parent.
Adapter error retry: each later completed attempt emits a new append-only brick_returned reply; old replies are not edited.
Hold then COO/human forward or stop: disposition_applied emits its own thread reply with the coo/human stamp voice.
Fan-in: gate phrase should distinguish 합류 대기 from 합류 성립 without treating either as success or quality.
Explicit policy with brick event kinds under building grain should validate vocabulary but not create thread replies unless grain is brick.
Provider ok false or network exception remains status-class-only evidence; no response body is persisted.

invariants: BRICK_REPORT_GRAIN absent or building means current notify-v2 behavior and Slack payloads stay byte-identical.
BRICK_REPORT_GRAIN=brick enables thread replies only when a building_started parent ts was recorded in the building root.
Notification exceptions never break receipt, return, link, disposition, or accumulated evidence writes.
Thread ts is read per send from building evidence, not cached in sender state.
Brick-grain Slack requests must include thread_ts; removing that attachment should make the focused checker fail.
Temp/non-vessel building roots must not invoke external Slack for any new event kind.
not_proven: No implementation was performed in this read-only design pass.
No checker, compileall, git diff --check, mutation RED, or temp-copy full gate was run in this pass.
Exact current StepOutputObservation raw-row body shape was not inspected because the bounded reading list excluded support/recording/step_outputs.py.
Exact receipt timestamp availability remains not_proven; implementation may need an in-memory received_at observation around adapter dispatch to render 받음(HH:MM).
Real Slack delivery reliability, provider response shape, reader notice, production behavior, source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, and Movement authority remain not_proven.
observed_evidence: Read-only inspection only; no files edited or created.
Repo root observed as /Users/smith/projects/BRICK-wt-notify3; git status --short produced no visible output.
notify-v2 currently admits only building_started, intervention_required, and building_finished as Building event kinds.
Slack sink currently posts chat.postMessage with channel and text only; it parses provider status class but does not retain ts/channel from the response.
run.py and walker_kernel.py already emit building start and terminal/hold events through best-effort wrappers that catch notification exceptions so evidence writes continue.
Step close evidence is written through _write_step_output_on_step_close / record_step_output after Agent return and gate-sequence decision attachment.

proposed_changes: In reporter.py, add BRICK_REPORT_GRAIN parsing with values building|brick; default/absent remains building.
Extend admitted event vocabulary with brick_received, brick_returned, gate_passed, and disposition_applied while preserving DEFAULT_BUILDING_EVENT_KINDS as the current three kinds.

Add optional brick-grain packet fields for step sequence number, step ref, attempt index, received_at, returned_at, one-line return summary, gate phrase, fan-in phrase, and report_grain; keep them provider-neutral and non-authoritative.
In report_sinks.py, record parent thread observation only after building_started Slack delivery returns ok with a ts; write ts/channel refs to the building root without raw response body.

In report_sinks.py, for brick-grain Slack replies read the recorded thread ts from the building root and include thread_ts in chat.postMessage; if missing, return not_attempted and never post brick events to the channel.
Do not additionally mirror intervention_required or building_finished into the thread in this slice; current channel behavior stays clean and regression parity remains narrower.
In run.py and walker_kernel.py, emit brick_returned after the step-output write, using best-effort delivery and appending observations to the existing _report_event_observations side channel.
In walker_kernel.py, emit disposition_applied after the resumed disposition is consumed and persisted through the accumulated evidence write.
Keep G6 sink refs unchanged; do not add sinks, scheduler, queue, retry, timers, dependencies, token caching, or sender-local thread state.
relevant_current_structure: support/operator/reporter.py validates report_event_policy, admitted event kinds, report packet shape, root vessel gating, and event packet rendering.
support/operator/report_sinks.py owns admitted sink dispatch, Slack request construction, external-vessel guard, forbidden packet fields, and status-class-only sink observations.
support/operator/run.py owns linear and single-step walk seams, step-output writes, and current building-level event emission.
support/operator/walker_kernel.py owns dynamic graph step close, gate transition, fan-in/hold/reroute flow, and resume disposition application.
support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py already houses reporter_notification_projection pins for Slack voice, auto-wiring, no-scheduler checks, vessel guard, forbidden fields, and G6 sink ceiling.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No brick/, agent/, link/, or project/ source edits are part of this design.
No new BAL fact class, Movement literal, Link route choice, GateFact authority, success judgment, or quality judgment.
No Slack token/channel value in report packets or generic sink observations.
No real Slack post in checker/proof; captured sender only.
No new sink beyond local-inbox, operator-wake-local, slack, and dashboard.
